request.question: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?

title: Santigold discography
The Explorers Club (band)
EggHunt Records
Andros Rodriguez
Against All Will
Santigold
Noah Georgeson
The Shortwave Set
Sarah Perrotta
Richard Davies (musician)
sentences: Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.
White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.
All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.
"Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.
The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.
The Explorers Club are a Pop rock band originally from the coast of South Carolina.
They are a 5-man band whose debut album was released by Dead Oceans.
The music of their first album is heavily influenced by the vocal harmony styles and production of The Beach Boys.
They are also influenced by classic rock and roll arrangements as made popular by The Beatles, Chuck Berry, Phil Spector, The Zombies, The Monkees and The Byrds.
Their second album shows more influences of soft-pop artists of the early-1970s such as Burt Bacharach, Glen Campbell, etc.
The band relocated to Nashville, TN in 2014 and released their third studio album Together on June 24, 2016
EggHunt Records is an independent record label based in Richmond, Virginia.
The label was founded by Adam Henceroth and Gregory Gendron in spring of 2014.
EggHunt Records focuses on upcoming, independent artists and releases material on cassette, CD, and vinyl physical formats, as well as digital download and streaming online.
EggHunt Records maintains an active roster of artists who have toured with notable acts such as The War on Drugs and Houndmouth.
EggHunt Records alumni include Lucy Dacus, whose debut album, "No Burden", was released on EggHunt Records in 2016.
In 2017, EggHunt Records signed Eric Slick of Dr. Dog to release his solo debut album, "Palisades".
Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.

His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Against All Will is a rock quartet from Los Angeles, California, whose debut album lineup was formed in Spring 2009 by Jimmy Allen (formerly of Puddle of Mudd), Jeff Current (formerly of Seven Story Drop), Steve "Boomstick" Wilson (formerly of Dead Kennedys and t.A.T.u.), and Cello Dias (formerly of Soulfly).
Songs "All About You" and "The Drug I Need" from their debut album entitled A Rhyme & Reason ranked in the national rock radio top 50 in 2010.
Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.
Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.
Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.
In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".
Noah Georgeson is a Grammy winning musician, producer, and solo recording artist.
Georgeson's debut album "Find Shelter" was released through Plain Recordings on November 28, 2006.
Born in San Anselmo, California, he moved with his family to Nevada City, California at the age of three.

Georgeson studied classical guitar and music composition, receiving his BA in composition from San Francisco State University in 2001, and, with a recommendation from Terry Riley, he attended Mills College, receiving his Master of Fine Arts in 2003.
While at Mills, Georgeson studied with Fred Frith, Pauline Oliveros, and Alvin Curran.
Georgeson first found popular success as a part of San Francisco band The Pleased, along with fellow member Joanna Newsom, whose debut album "The Milk-Eyed Mender" he produced.

As a musician, producer, and mixer, Georgeson has since worked with Devendra Banhart, The Strokes, Little Joy, Bert Jansch, Charlotte Gainsbourg, Robin Pecknold, Mason Jennings, Cedric Bixler, Adam Green, Os Mutantes, Adan Jodorowsky, Harper Simon, Flo Morrissey, Cate Le Bon, and Rodrigo Amarante.
The Shortwave Set are a British alternative pop band whose debut album, "The Debt Collection", was released in 2005 on Independiente Records.
The band's music combines sample-based music with more traditional songwriting and instrumentation to create a sound the group have described as "Victorian Funk".
The band's second album, "Replica Sun Machine", was released on 12 May 2008 by Wall of Sound.
Sarah Perrotta (born 1979, United States) is a Hudson Valley based singer, songwriter and pianist.
Perrotta was the front woman of the indie-rock band Outloud Dreamer whose debut album “Drink The Sky” was named best modern rock album of 2000 by WKZE Radio 98.1FM.
Perrotta’s second album “The Well” was self-released in 2008 and features Tony Levin on bass and Garth Hudson on accordion.
The album was mixed by Grammy Award winner Malcolm Burn.
Her most recent record is entitled tonight released under her surname.
Richard Davies is an Australian-American musician.
He first came to prominence in the early 1990s as leader of the Australian band The Moles.
Upon moving to the United States, Davies joined with Eric Matthews to form Cardinal, whose debut album, "Cardinal," was released by Flydaddy in 1994.
He has since released several solo albums including "There's Never Been a Crowd Like This" (1996), "Telegraph" (1998), and "Barbarians" (2000).
In 2009 Davies and Guided by Voices front man Robert Pollard, collaborating under the name Cosmos, released an album, "Jar of Jam / Ton of Bricks."
In 2012 Davies and Matthews reformed Cardinal and released a second album, "Hymns."

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Role: This agent summarizes first-hop evidence and exposes the bridge entity, any missing fact(s), ambiguity, and concrete next-hop retrieval directions. Do not attempt to produce the final answer—the summary should tell the next agent exactly what to fetch to finish the multi-hop chain.

Output requirements (single `summary` value only):
- Start the summary with the header: First-hop findings:
- Include these labeled parts (use a short bullet list or 2–4 concise sentences; keep overall output brief):
  1) Evidence found: For each person/entity named in the question, list which passage(s) mention matching candidate pages/titles and quote any explicit fact tokens found there (names, birth years, publication years, short parenthetical notes). Keep quoted fragments <=15 words and cite the passage title/label for each fact.
  2) Ambiguities: If a name yields multiple candidate pages or variants in the passages, explicitly enumerate those candidate titles/labels as they appear; if an expected page/variant is missing, note that too.
  3) Bridge entity / Missing fact: Explicitly name the bridge entity exactly as it appears in the passages (the person, work, or organization that links to the next hop) and state the precise missing fact needed to answer the question (e.g., a birth date, founding year, canonical series ordering).
  4) Retrieval direction: Provide 2–4 concrete, prioritized next-hop targets (specific page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, and/or exact sections to check such as lede/infobox/Career/History). Include short example search keywords or one-line instructions (e.g., "open '<Name>' page and check lede/infobox and 'Career' section for 'Italian champion' year").
- End with a brief explicit note that the final answer is deferred until those pages are retrieved.

Hard constraints (do not violate):
- Do not provide a final comparison or answer here. Do not guess or invent facts not present in the provided passages or introduce outside knowledge.
- Do not assume which candidate is intended when multiple candidates exist.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passages (>15 words).
- Preserve concise factual phrasing and verbatim tokens that appear in the passages (names, dates, page titles) when reporting evidence.

Keep the summary concise and focused—just the first-hop findings needed to guide the next retrieval hop.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Output contract:
- Produce exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short retrieval query string (one line, no surrounding explanation or extra fields).
- Do not answer, summarize, or explain anything in `query`. Do not include fallback instructions or user-facing requests for more info in this field.

Construction rules (apply the most specific rule that fits the inputs):
1) Linking two named works: If `question` or `summary_1` names two or more specific works (songs, tracks, titles) and the missing evidence is the bridge between them, emit a concise interrogative that names both works and requests the linking evidence (artist / album / recording). Keep it short (about ≤ 12 words). Example form: "Which artist or album recorded both 'Work A' and 'Work B'?"
2) Missing album artist/genre: If `summary_1` indicates the album title is present but the artist or genre is unknown, emit a compact search string of the form: <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
   - No punctuation, no question phrasing, no extra words. Example: "Levitate album artist genre"
3) Sports season-year lookups: If the `question` contains a specific season-year token and the missing bridge is a league-season page, emit a short query that includes the year token, a league identifier (e.g., "NHL" or "National Hockey League"), and a cue such as "regular season" or "season count". Keep it concise (≤ ~10 tokens).
4) Default: Otherwise, produce one concise search-style query (≤ ~12 words) that uses visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` targeted at retrieving the missing supporting evidence (artist, album, recording, genre, season page, etc.).

General guidance:
- Keep queries compact; avoid lists, long page titles, or copying large spans from hidden targets.
- Do not invent facts or candidate answers; use only visible tokens or neutral retrieval cues.
- Prefer a short search string; use an interrogative only for the two-works linking case in rule 1.
- The goal is to maximize recovery of the missing bridge evidence for the downstream retriever.

Return only the single `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Primary function: integrate the newly retrieved `passages` (second-hop evidence) with the provided `context` (first-hop) so the output is a grounded post-retrieval state that preserves the facts the final-answer generator needs. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when evidence is insufficient.

Behavior rules (minimal, general):
1) Preserve concise-answer behavior. If `context` already contains a concise direct answer (a short sentence or phrase), keep that text exactly as the first sentence of `summary`, unless it contradicts the `passages`.
2) When `passages` supply additional, directly supported facts about entities named in the `question` or `context`, append up to one brief supporting sentence per distinct named entity (keep total `summary` to 1–3 sentences, preferably 1–2). Each supporting sentence should: start with the entity's full name as shown in the passages, include a short paraphrased attribution drawn only from the passages (no more than ~10–12 words) such as nationality, a notable achievement, a birth date, or a current affiliation, and avoid inventing or inferring facts not present in the inputs.
3) When an object/entity page (for example, a car page, group page, or similar) lists associated people and a separate passage provides a biography for one of those listed people, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary: (a) first sentence: name the people as listed on the object page (e.g., "driven by X and Y" or "member of Group Z"); (b) optional second short clause/sentence: paraphrase key biographical facts from the person's passage (only facts present there — e.g., nationality, notable wins/awards, birth date, current team/affiliation). Keep wording compact and factual.
4) Ground everything in the provided `passages` and `context`. Do not add new claims, dates, roles, or affiliations not explicitly supported. Avoid long verbatim quotations from the passages; you may paraphrase short phrases (≤10 words) only if they appear in the sources, and you may copy short strings such as names or dates exactly as shown.
5) If the passages do not provide enough evidence to resolve the bridge, state that the passages do not specify the requested fact while still listing any supported names or facts. Do not guess or fabricate missing information.
6) Output contract: return only the field `summary` containing 1–3 concise sentences (preferably 1–2) that integrate the first-hop `context` and second-hop `passages` according to the rules above. Do not add commentary, answer-labels, or extraneous text.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) together with the question as your evidence. Do not introduce external facts, sources, or claims not present in those summaries.
- Treat summary_2 (the later, integrated post-retrieval state) as primary evidence while still considering summary_1. Do not assume the final state is unresolved simply because summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge; rely on the combined visible summaries to reach a conclusion.
- If summary_1 and summary_2 both clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, then the value of the `answer` field must be exactly one lowercase token: either "yes" (if both summaries affirm the fact) or "no" (if both summaries deny the fact). The `answer` field must contain only that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no capitalization, no explanation, no additional words).
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not together provide a clear binary yes/no verdict, produce a concise answer string in the `answer` field that best reflects what the summaries support. This concise answer may be a short phrase or sentence, but must still be supported solely by the summaries and must avoid adding new facts.
- Always minimize verbosity: prefer the single-token binary response when the summaries unambiguously agree; otherwise keep the answer brief and directly tied to the visible summaries.

Produce only the `answer` field as output.

response.hop1_query: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?
hop1_docs: Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Andros Rodriguez | Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.  His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Cold Fact | Cold Fact is the debut album from American singer-songwriter Rodriguez.  It was released in the United States on the Sussex label in March 1970.  The album sold very poorly in the United States (Rodriguez was himself an unknown in the US), but managed to sell well in both South Africa and Australia, with Rodriguez touring Australia in 1979.

Red. White. Green. | Red.  White.  Green.  is the second studio album by American deathcore band Upon a Burning Body.  The album was released on March 26, 2012 through Sumerian Records.  The album was produced by Will Putney, who produced their debut album, "The World Is Ours".  All of the songs on the album are titled after films that Robert Rodriguez produced or directed, continuing a trend set from their debut album.  It is the only album with Jonathan Gonzalez on drums and the last album with Chris "CJ" Johnson on guitar.
My Versions | My Versions is the debut album of Swedish singer Robin Stjernberg who was formerly a member of the boyband What's Up!  with Eric Saade and had placed second in Idol 2011.
response.summary_1:
First-hop findings:
1) Evidence found:
- Singer identity: passage [1] — "Santi White ... stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold)" and "'Her debut album, 'Santogold' " (passage [1]).
- Discography note: passage [1] — "'Her second album, 'Master of My Make-Believe', was released in 2012.'" (passage [1]).
- Andros Rodriguez client list: passage [4] — "His diverse catalog of clients include ... Santigold" (passage [4]).
2) Ambiguities:
- Stage-name variants appear: "Santigold" vs "Santogold" vs given name "Santi White" (passage [1]); ensure which variant is used on the Andros Rodriguez client listing (passage [4] uses "Santigold").
- A dedicated page for "Master of My Make-Believe" is not included in the passages provided (present mention only in passage [1]).
3) Bridge entity / Missing fact:
- Bridge entity (as in the passages): "Santigold" (Santi White).
- Precise missing fact to confirm: authoritative verification that Santigold's second studio album is titled "Master of My Make-Believe" and its position/order in her discography (and optionally the release year).
4) Retrieval direction (prioritized):
- Open the "Santigold" (Santi White) artist page — check the lede/infobox and "Discography" or "Career" sections to confirm album ordering and titles.
  Example search: open "Santigold" page; check lede/infobox and "Discography" for "second album".
- Open the "Master of My Make-Believe" album page — check lede/infobox for album type (studio), release year, and statement that it is her second album.
  Example search: open "Master of My Make-Believe" page; verify "second studio album" and 2012 release.
- Re-open "Andros Rodriguez" page — check the clients list to confirm the spelling/match ("Santigold") and that this Santigold is the same artist (cross-check with linked artist page).
  Example search: open "Andros Rodriguez" page; find "clients include ... Santigold" and follow link.
- (Optional) Open "Santogold (album)" page to reconfirm the debut album details and date if needed.
  Example search: open "Santogold (album)" lede/infobox for release date and that it is the debut.
Final answer deferred until those pages/sections are retrieved and verified.
response.hop2_query: Which artist recorded both "Santogold" and "Master of My Make-Believe"?
hop2_docs: Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Master of My Make-Believe | Master of My Make-Believe is the second studio album by American recording artist Santigold.  It was released in the United Kingdom on April 24, 2012 and in the United States on May 1, 2012, by Downtown Records and Atlantic Records.  The cover art representing four incarnations of Santigold was designed by Jason Schmidt.  The record was produced with a wide range of different collaborators, songwriters and producers; including her long-time friends of Switch and Diplo, as well as David Andrew Sitek of TV on the Radio and Nick Zinner of the Yeah Yeah Yeahs.

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.

Disparate Youth | "Disparate Youth" is a song from American singer-songwriter Santigold.  The track was released in the United States on 14 February 2012 as the lead single from the artist's second studio album, "Master of My Make-Believe" (2012).

My Truth | My Truth is the second studio album by Swedish recording artist Robyn.  It was released on 17 May 1999 through BMG Sweden.  Robyn collaborated with producers including Lindström & Ekhé, Christian Falk, Billy Mann, Thomas Rusiak and Masters at Work during its recording.  A pop and R&B record, "My Truth" is an autobiographical album for which Robyn co-wrote all fourteen songs.  Certain lyrics reflect on the abortion the singer had in the fall of 1998, which sparked controversy when RCA Records began formulating a North American release.  When she refused to make alterations to the album per request by the label, the plans to release "My Truth" outside of Sweden were cancelled.

L.E.S. Artistes | "L.E.S. Artistes" is the second single by American artist Santigold (known then as "Santogold"), and along with "Creator", the first to be taken from her self-titled debut "Santogold" (see 2008 in music).

Believe Acoustic | Believe Acoustic is the third remix album by Canadian recording artist Justin Bieber.  It was released on January 29, 2013 through Island Records as the follow-up to his second remix album "" (2011).  This also marks the second acoustic album following "My Worlds Acoustic" (2010).  The album features acoustic and live versions of songs from his third studio album "Believe" (2012), as well as three new recordings.  The album went to number one on the US Billboard 200 chart making this Bieber's fifth number-one album and seventh consecutive top 10 album in the United States.
response.summary_2: Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012. Santigold (Santi White, born September 25, 1976) is an American singer.
